A solid circular bar is subjected to pure torsion. Which of the following measures will reduce the maximum torsional deformation in the bar?

Q2 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A thin tyre of steel is to be mounted onto a rigid wheel of 2 m diameter, using an interference fit after fitting the tyre experience hoop stress of 120 MPa. Assuming elastic behaviour and taking the modulus of elasticity of steel as 200 GPa, the internal diameter of the tyre is ______ m.
[Correct upto two decimal places]

NAT
Correct answer: 1.99
Solution

1.99 (1.9 - 2.0)

 
Q3 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Three identical pin-connected bars support a load P as shown in figure. All the bars are of the same area of cross-section and same length. Neglect the possibility of lateral buckling of the bar. The vertical displacement of the point where the load is applied will be

 
 
Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
Q4 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A rigid horizontal bar AB hinged at A is supported by 1 m long steel rod and 2 m long bronze rod, both rigidly fixed at the upper ends. A load of 50 kN is applied at point 4 m from the hinge point A. The areas of cross-section of the steel and bronze rods are 800 mm2 and 600 mm2,
respectively, then the reaction at point A is
[Take, Es = 200 GPa and Eb = 80 GPa]

 
 
Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

 
Q5 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A steel tube of 40 mm outer diameter and 35 mm inner diameter encloses a gun metal rod of 25 mm diameter. The assembly is rigidly joined at each end. At a temperature of 30°C there is no longitudinal stress in either material. If the if the temperature of assembly is raised to 200°C, and the coefficient of thermal expansion of steel is 10 × 10–6/°C, coefficient of thermal expansion of gun metal is 20 × 10–6/°C, 200 GPa, modulus of elasticity of gun metal is 90 GPa, then the stress induced in the gun metal is _____ MPa. [Correct upto two decimal places]

NAT
Correct answer: -87.428
Solution

-87.428 (-88 - -86)

 
Q6 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A rigid block AB weighing 200 kN is supported by three rods symmetrically as shown in figure.
Before attaching the weight, the lower ends of the rods are set at the same level. The areas of cross-section of the steel and copper rods are 1000 mm2 and 2000 mm2 respectively.
Given : Ec = 90 GPa; αc = 18 × 10–6/°C; Es = 200 GPa; αs = 11 × 10–6/°C.
If the temperature is raised by 20°C, then stress in copper rod is

 
 
Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
Q7 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Three wires of the same material and cross-section support a rigid bar which further supports a weight of 10 kN. The length of the wires is 4 m, 8 m and 10 m as shown in figure.

 
Which of the following statements are correct?
Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(b, c, d)

 
Q8 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A square steel bar of dimension 40 mm × 40 mm × 200 mm is subjected to an axial load of 500 kN.
If the one-third of the lateral strain is prevented by applying uniform pressure on the rectangular surfaces, then the decrease in length of the bar will be ____ mm. [Take μ = 0.3 and E = 200 GPa]
(Correct up to three decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 0.286
Solution

0.286 (0.270 - 0.290)

 
 
Q9 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Two pieces of wood of section 60 mm × 20 mm are joined together along a plane inclined at 60° to the x-axis. If the strength of the joint is 10 MPa in tension and 5 MPa in shear, the maximum force which the member can sustain is _______ kN. [Correct upto two decimal places]

NAT
Correct answer: 13.856
Solution

13.856 (13.7 - 13.9)

Correct answer: 0.37
Solution
